Um, so the thing with supplementation, um, look at nutrition as a triage, you know, like energy balance, most important thing. Right. And then macro nutrient breakdown of those calories and then micro nutrients of the quality of the food, then nutrient timing. So that's when people get into like carb cycling or different stuff like that. And then lastly, supplementation. So research is like, it's very validating that supplements help results, but only at the very tip when all the other stuff is taken care of. So we actually have our own brand of supplements that we engineered because people were getting supplements elsewhere and then getting proteins that had sugar in it, not knowing it or, uh, because the FDA doesn't regulate the supplement industry. So we do have our own. Yeah. So we do have our own, uh, cause now we know what's in them. Um, but you don't have to get ours. You get someone else's, uh, or you don't even have to do the supplements. Um, they just. So are you, is that built into the plan to have some kind of supplement or it's mostly just built the only thing built into the plan is a protein shake.
